channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Chitral Valley Faces Rising Flood Risks Amid Glacial Melt and Climate Change
item.description: As summer is nearing, Chitral valley is once again under threat of severe flooding. Residents and experts are calling for urgent construction of continuous bulwarks along the Chitral River to protect ...

item.title: Forest department initiates rescue as wild elephant falls into unused well near Sullia
item.description: On the night of April 12, 2026, a wild elephant accidentally fell into an unused open well at Kalcharpe, near Peraje, under the Sullia forest range in Dakshina Kannada district, Karnataka. The well is...

item.title: Blue Jays Dropped by Twins as Scherzer Struggles in Early Season
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays suffered their fourth consecutive series loss after being defeated 8-2 by the Minnesota Twins on Sunday. Veteran pitcher Max Scherzer, a future Hall of Famer, had a rough outing,...

item.title: Islamabad Red Zone and Main Roads Closed Amid US-Iran Delegation Visit
item.description: On Sunday, Islamabad police announced a complete closure of the Red Zone and adjoining areas, restricting access solely to official vehicles. Traffic from Kashmir Chowk towards Serena Chowk and Srinag...

item.title: RIAT College Student Found Dead in Kisumu, Ex-Boyfriend Arrested Amid Investigation
item.description: A 21-year-old female student from RIAT College was discovered dead in Riat Forest, Kisumu West, days after she was reported missing. The Directorate of Criminal Investigations (DCI) confirmed that the...

item.title: Fact-check of key claims from the BBC Scotland leaders' debate ahead of the 2026 election
item.description: BBC Verify has examined several claims made by party leaders during a 90-minute televised debate ahead of the Scottish Parliament election. SNP leader John Swinney stated that energy bills would be £7...

item.title: Delayed breast reconstruction leaves UK women in prolonged physical and emotional distress
item.description: During the Covid-19 pandemic, numerous women in the UK underwent urgent mastectomies without immediate breast reconstruction, a procedure usually performed simultaneously. The NHS paused these complex...

item.title: US-Iran Talks Conclude Without Breakthrough, Ceasefire Commitment Remains
item.description: Recent negotiations between the United States and Iran, aimed at de-escalating regional tensions, have concluded without a decisive breakthrough. While no significant progress was achieved, both sides...

item.title: TCS confirms suspension of employees amid harassment allegations at Nashik office
item.description: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), a major IT company, has reiterated its long-standing zero-tolerance policy against harassment and coercion following allegations at its Nashik office. On April 12, 202...

item.title: CPPE challenges World Bank’s call for more fuel and food imports, says Nigeria should strengthen local production
item.description: The Centre for the Promotion of Private Enterprise (CPPE) has strongly opposed the World Bank’s suggestion that Nigeria should increase its importation of fuel and food to solve supply problems. In it...

item.title: US military to implement limited blockade of Iranian ports amid failed Pakistan-mediated talks
item.description: The United States military announced it will begin a blockade of Iranian ports starting Monday, following the collapse of high-level negotiations in Pakistan. President Donald Trump attributed the bre...

item.title: High School Students Build Robotic Birds to Support Sage Grouse Population at Grand Teton National Park
item.description: At Grand Teton National Park, high school students, guided by robotics mentor Gary Duquette, have developed robotic bird decoys to help restore the declining sage grouse population. These robotic bird...

item.title: US Influencer Ashlee Jenae Dies in Zanzibar during Vacation, Family Seeks Justice
item.description: American social media influencer Ashlee Jenae, whose real name was Ashly Robinson, has tragically died while on holiday in Zanzibar, Tanzania. The 31-year-old was celebrating both her birthday and her...

item.title: Interior Secretary Remulla confirms no Interpol red notice has been issued for Harry Roque
item.description: Interior Secretary Juanito Victor 'Jonvic' Remulla clarified on April 13, 2026, that former presidential spokesperson Harry Roque currently does not have an Interpol red notice issued against him, eve...

item.title: Péter Magyar Wins Hungarian Election, Ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-Year Rule
item.description: Hungary’s long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán conceded defeat in the country’s parliamentary elections, ending a 16-year era of nationalist and increasingly authoritarian governance. The oppositi...

item.title: Cotabato City experiences extreme heat with 42°C heat index
item.description: Cotabato City is currently experiencing extreme heat, with the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) forecasting a heat index of 42°C on Monday, April 1...

item.title: Labour rights violations reported in Pakistan's textile recycling industry supplying global fashion brands
item.description: A recent study by the Labour Education Foundation (LEF) in collaboration with Arisa, a Dutch rights organisation, has highlighted serious labour rights issues in Pakistan's textile recycling sector. T...

item.title: Gauteng Health outlines measures to reduce surgical backlog and improve hospital capacity
item.description: The Gauteng Department of Health has announced a comprehensive plan to reduce a significant surgical backlog of about 30,600 patients in the province’s public hospitals. Of this total, almost 30,000 c...

item.title: Kerala LoP VD Satheesan writes to Election Commission urging prompt release of detailed polling data from April 9 assembly elections
item.description: In a letter dated April 12, 2026, and shared on social media, V D Satheesan, the Leader of Opposition in the Kerala Legislative Assembly and senior Congress leader, has urged the Election Commission o...

item.title: Borno Governor Urges Residents Not to Aid Boko Haram, Highlights Military Operations
item.description: Borno State Governor, Babagana Zulum, don talk say make people no dey help, hide, or give any support to Boko Haram insurgents. Dis warning come after recent air operation wey Joint Task Force (North ...
